title: Hoppea fastigiata

The family Ericaceae is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as the heath family. These plants are typically found in acidic soils and are often associated with heathlands and moorlands. Hoppea fastigiata is a relatively rare and endemic species found in limited geographic areas.
uses: Hoppea fastigiata, also known as the Hoppea tree, has been found to have various medicinal properties in traditional African medicine. The plant's roots, bark, and leaves are used to treat different health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations.
